summary: Patient app that records doctor visits and turns them into clear next steps.
editor_note: The bet is that the missing layer in care is memory, not another portal. Kin records doctor visits, extracts next steps, and lets a patient share the summary with family before the next appointment. Unlike clinician scribes, it starts with the person who forgets 80% of instructions by the parking lot. Free patient distribution is elegant; consent norms in exam rooms are the hard part.

product_evidence.claim: Official product positioning describes Kin Health: Patient app that records doctor visits and turns them into clear next steps.
VentureDex product review notes that unlike clinician scribes, it starts with the person who forgets 80% of instructions by the parking lot.

market_context.primary_user: Healthcare, benefits, or life-sciences teams evaluating regulated operational workflows.
market_context.why_now: Recent source-backed funding and public product evidence make healthtech, patient notes, care coordination worth tracking.

risks.claim: Free patient distribution is elegant; consent norms in exam rooms are the hard part.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
